edits.slides.0.description: The patient is in the chair. The scanner is ready. The system isn't. I redesigned Align Technology's RX, Scan, and View workflow — three tools built separately, but used by clinicians as one appointment. The same kind of handoff problem shows up in any workflow product that outgrew its original design.
edits.slides.4.content: I mapped the RX → Scan → View flow as clinicians lived it. The same problem showed up at each transition.
edits.slides.4.highlight: Mapping the flow showed where clinicians lost time, and gave each goal a specific problem to fix.
edits.slides.8.content: The old setup was a multi-step form — patient details and procedure locked behind a blocking modal before anything could start.
edits.slides.13.issues.2: Every new feature added more for clinicians to remember
edits.slides.21.whatWorked.0: The coded prototype caught timing failures Figma never would have.
edits.slides.21.whatYoudDoDifferently.1: Run toolbar testing before wireframes — placement ripples into everything.
